artPrompt: An evocative scene of a town unwittingly fattening its monster: a poisoned reservoir bulges and ripples as faint hands from every edge of the frame let coins, dice, and slips drift into the murk, the water glowing sickly bioluminescent as a vast shape stirs beneath. Rusted pipes drip above under a bruised sky. Wide landscape, murky green and rust-red washes bleeding into deep teal. Rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Everyone Feeds It Whether They Mean To
text:
If a player would roll a die, flip a coin, or vote, instead they do that and you put a sludge counter on a creature you control AND that player puts a sludge counter on a creature you control. (Yes, twice. The town's chaos feeds it coming and going.)
{R}{G}, Sacrifice Everyone Feeds It Whether They Mean To: Each opponent loses life equal to the most sludge counters on a creature you control.
flavorText: The PSA said "do not feed the reservoir." Reading the PSA out loud counted as feeding it.
